articles.title: THE BIGGEST TEAMS IN CRYPTO ARE DITCHING SEED PHRASES
articles.summary: Why? Because they can use smart wallets [4] to bring new users onchain with just an email and eliminate gas fees entirely.

articles.title: ↗️ Get up to $25K in credits to get started
BITCOIN TRADES AT $105,500, LONG-TERM OUTLOOK REMAINS OPTIMISTIC
articles.summary: Bitcoin is trading at around $105.5k after pulling back from its $111.8k all-time high. Analysts are calling this a key pivot zone that could determine the next leg of the market cycle. BTC Markets' Rachael Lucas says a push above $105K could target $115K, while a drop below $103K risks a pullback to the $93K–$97K range—without breaking the broader uptrend. Institutional flows continue to dominate, sidelining altcoins and positioning Bitcoin as a strategic macro hedge in corporate treasuries.

articles.title: CIRCLE RAISES IPO TARGET TO $896M AMID STRONG INVESTOR INTEREST
articles.summary: Circle increased its IPO offering to 32 million shares at a $27–$28 price range, raising its target proceeds to $896 million, up from 24 million shares at $24–$26. The boost follows hints of a $6.7 billion valuation, up from $5 billion, and interest from major institutions like BlackRock, which may take a 10% stake. This surge in appetite coincides with growing stablecoin adoption, with $94.2 billion settled between January 2023 and February 2025, and a more favorable U.S. regulatory landscape under the Trump administration, including progress on the CLARITY Act and evolving SEC and CFTC guidance.

articles.title: AAVE STAKING AND RISK SYSTEM OVERHAUL
articles.summary: Aave governance has passed the Umbrella activation, launching a new staking and slashing system to replace the legacy Safety Module. The upgrade introduces high-yield aToken staking (up to 12% on stables), phases out StkGHO, cuts AAVE emissions, and automates bad debt resolution making Aave more capital-efficient, lower-cost, and better protected. The new vault architecture will roll out in three days.

articles.title: ORBITAL, AN AMM FOR POOLS OF 2, 3, OR 10,000 STABLECOINS
articles.summary: Orbital generalizes concentrated liquidity to pools of three or more stablecoins by organizing “ticks” as nested spherical caps around the $1 equal-price point, enabling LPs to allocate capital where stablecoins trade most tightly. Each tick's reserves lie on an n-dimensional sphere intersected by a hyperplane (the tick boundary), and interior ticks behave like full spheres while boundary ticks reduce to (n−1)-dimensional spheres. By decomposing total reserves into components parallel and orthogonal to the equal-price vector, Orbital derives a toroidal invariant that lets it compute trades and handle tick boundary crossings in constant time regardless of the pool's dimension. This design delivers significant capital efficiency, allowing a 5-coin pool with a 1% depeg tolerance to see ~150× efficiency while allowing LPs to customize exposure across multiple depeg scenarios.

articles.title: THE END OF THE FOUNDATION ERA IN CRYPTO
articles.summary: Crypto foundations, once useful for navigating early regulatory hostility, now impose misaligned incentives, legal constraints, and centralizing inefficiencies. With emerging “control‐based” regulatory frameworks, developer companies can more effectively deploy capital, attract talent, and align with tokenholders via tools like revenue sharing, milestone vesting, and contractual safeguards. Decentralized Unincorporated Nonprofit Associations (DUNAs) and on‐chain governance tooling (BORGs) offer lightweight, transparent alternatives to foundation structures by enabling DAOs to contract and enforce obligations without opaque intermediaries. As a result, companies and DAOs can achieve true decentralization, accountability, and scale without relying on slow nonprofit foundations.

articles.title: THE DEATH OF INFOFI
articles.summary: InfoFi platforms like Kaito profit massively by charging projects high listing fees and taking cuts from token taxes, effectively selling user attention to bidders. While companies gain temporary mindshare, they suffer from inauthentic engagement, AI-generated low‑quality content, poor conversion, and post‑campaign token sell‑offs that harm credibility. This model generates fatigue and burnout among both creators and genuine projects that can't afford or don't want to participate. InfoFi platforms should iterate and improve their algorithms, rewards, and conversion mechanisms to foster more authentic, sustainable engagement.

articles.title: STABLECOIN MARKET CAPITALIZATION SURPASSES $250 BILLION
articles.summary: The total stablecoin market cap has surpassed $250 billion for the first time, driven by regulatory momentum and surging DeFi adoption. USDT leads with $153B, followed by USDC at $60.9B, amid the advancement of the U.S. GENIUS Act and Hong Kong's licensing framework. With TradFi firms eyeing joint stablecoin ventures and DEX volumes hitting record shares, analysts expect the stablecoin market could double by 2026.

articles.title: YOU PROBABLY SHOULDN'T DO CRYPTO IN 2025
articles.summary: Crypto Twitter is saturated with shillers and extractive tactics, making genuine alpha nearly impossible to find from popular accounts. Newcomers should avoid buying tokens based on public endorsements, especially from large profiles, as most are paid or have undisclosed positions. Airdrop farming remains one of the cleaner ways to participate, and platforms like Kaito can help smaller voices get noticed more ethically. Ultimately, succeed by spending more time learning, curating your feed, and risking less capital.

articles.title: LARGEST AIRDROPS OF 2025
articles.summary: Berachain led 2025's airdrop wave with a $679M distribution, followed by KaitoAI and Story Protocol at ~$200M and ~$190M, respectively.

articles.title: HYPERLIQUID CRITICIZED FOR MISLEADING "DECENTRALIZED" CLAIMS
articles.summary: Despite branding itself as a decentralized exchange, Hyperliquid is under fire for housing all user funds in a 3/4 team-controlled multisig on Arbitrum, prompting accusations of deceptive marketing.

articles.title: HYPERLIQUID WILL NOT FLIP BINANCE
articles.summary: Hyperliquid's speed bump design protects makers but limits its potential to surpass Binance in volume.

articles.title: ACTIVE STABLECOIN ADDRESSES HITS ATH
articles.summary: May saw a record 33.1 million active stablecoin addresses, while Circle's CCTP bridge hit an all-time high of $7.7B in volume up 83.3% from April.

articles.title: ETHEREUM LEADS $286M WEEKLY INFLOWS AS BITCOIN ETF STREAK ENDS
articles.summary: Crypto funds saw $286M in net inflows last week, marking seven straight weeks of gains, with Ethereum products pulling in $321M their best run since 2024.
